rulings.Case Title: MARY NICHOLS VS. MARK STREHLOW ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mark Strehlow'S Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Documents Of Hendrick Bartel
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 9, 2018, Line 9, Defendant Mark Strehlow's Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Documents Of Hendrick Bartel Pro Tem Judge Conor Moore, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing Required. Parties should be prepared to address the timeliness of the filing and service of the motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sfjudgeprotem@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MICHELLE FULLER VS. SPECIALIZED LOAN SERVICING, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Reopen Discovery Pursuant To Ccp 2024.050(A); Memorandum Of Points And Authorities.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 9, 2018, Line 1, PLAINTIFF MICHELLE FULLER'S Motion To Reopen Discovery Pursuant To Ccp 2024.050(A) Pro Tem Judge Conor Moore, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice is Granted. Plaintiff's motion to reopen discovery is Denied. The Court's order granting Plaintiff leave to amend her complaint explicitly stated that Plaintiff may amend her complaint "alleging any statutory cause of action based on the same facts." Plaintiff amended her complaint based on the same facts previously alleged. Plaintiff took written discovery and depositions while discovery was open, and there is no good cause shown for why Plaintiff needs additional discovery to prepare for trial. The parties' requests for sanctions are Denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sfjudgeprotem@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative rulingAny party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sfjudgeprotem@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CHARU SAROJ DAVE ET AL VS. 2121 LAGUNA STREET CONDOMINIUM HOMEOWNERS'S ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Defendants Special Interrogatories, Set Two And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 9, 2018, Line 2, DEFENDANTS 2121 LAGUNA STREET CONDOMINIUM HOMEOWNERS'S, RAND RICHARDS' Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Defendants Special Interrogatories, Set Two And Request For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Conor Moore, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants' motion to compel is Granted. Defendants properly served two special interrogatories on Plaintiff Shannon Gallagher. Plaintiff did not respond to the special interrogatories. Instead, Plaintiff replied with a letter claiming that there was no need to respond because the special interrogatories were not submitted alongside the required declaration. See Cal. Civ. Proc. Code 2050.030. This objection, although not in a formal response, has no merit because Defendants did serve the special interrogatories with the required declaration. Defendants are entitled to code-compliant responses to its discovery. Plaintiff shall serve code-compliant responses, along with any privilege log, on Defendants no later than November 16, 2018. Normally, this conduct would result in sanctions, but Plaintiff's counsel states that she did not receive the discovery until September 26. See Gallagher Decl. 12. Accepting this claim on good faith, Defendants' request for sanctions is Denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sfjudgeprotem@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative rulingAny party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sfjudgeprotem@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: HUONG (AMY) THAI-SILVA VS. MINH THAI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Response To Special Interrogatories, Set One, Number 6; Request For Sanctions Against Defendant And Their Attorney, Tannner, Brink, In The Amount Of $942
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 9, 2018, Line 3, PLAINTIFFS HUONG (AMY) THAI-SILVA, VINH THAI, MUOI QUACH'S Motion To Compel Further Response To Special Interrogatories, Set One, Number 6; Request For Sanctions Against Defendant And Their Attorney, Tannner, Brink, In The Amount Of $942 Pro Tem Judge Conor Moore, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Palintiff Huong (Amy) Thai-Silva's motion to compel further responses to Special Interrogatory number 6 is Denied. Defendant Minh Thai responded to the special interrogatory about his annual income in 1999. Defendant does not recall his income in that year and was unable to locate the information after a "reasonable and good faith effort" to obtain it. Although Plaintiff doubts the sincerity of Defendant's responses (See Reply Brief, "it is very suspicious that defendant is 'unable' to state his income" for 1999), there is nothing further the Court can compel Defendant to do or say in response to Plaintiff's special interrogatory. Defendant served a timely, code-compliant response to Plaintiff. Plaintiff filed this motion without substantial justification or a good faith basis for doing so. Accordingly, Defendant's request for sanctions is Granted. Plaintiff and her counsel are sanctioned, jointly and severally, in the amount of $1,000, payable to Defendant no later than December 10, 2018. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sfjudgeprotem@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: PMGI FINANCIAL, LLC. VS. CHRISTINA J. JOHNSON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 9, 2018, Line 9, PLAINTIFF PMGI FINANCIAL, LLC. HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION The claim of exemption is GRANTED IN PART. The court concludes that the account contained "paid earnings" that are exempt per CCP 704.070(b)(2). The court finds that $1,482.07 of the money levied upon is exempt. The creditor shall return that sum to defendant as the papers indicate that the Sheriff is not holding the funds. rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 9, 2018, Line 6, PLAINTIFF MUHAMMAD KHAN'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery Pro Tem Judge Conor Moore, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion to compel is Granted. Defendants' answer, and amended answer, both state as an affirmative defense that Defendants are not liable because they acted "in reasonable and good faith reliance" on their counsel. The court in Aetna held that it the defendant did not waive attorney-client privilege because it did not "in fact rely[] on an 'advice of counsel' defense." See 153 Cal.App.3d 467, 475. Instead, the defendant in Aetna simply asserted that it denied the insurance claim because there was no valid claim on the facts. Defendants' affirmative defense, however, states that they are not liable because of good faith reliance on their counsel, which is different from claiming that the Defendants took action because it was lawful for them to do so. Introducing "reasonable and good faith" implies that Defendants are not liable because they acted in good faith, not because they acted correctly. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is Denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sfjudgeprotem@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: BETHANY SHERMAN VS. DOES 1 TO 10
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Subpoena Duces Tecum And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 9, 2018, Line 7, OTHER JOHN DOE, JANE DOE Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Subpoena Duces Tecum And For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Conor Moore, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Doe Defendants have labeled Plaintiff as a "neo-Nazi" with a "white supremacist" twitter account who believes in the "the Jewish conspiracy." These labels expose Plaintiff "to hatred, contempt, ridicule, or obloquy, or which causes [her] to be sunned or avoided, or which has a tendency to injure [her] in [her] occupation." (See Dickinson v. Cosby (2017) 17 Cal.App.5th 655, 685. Plaintiff alleges harm to her business and reputation, which Defendants concede given than, as they put it, "[i]t is no secret that most people deplore Nazism¿." (See MPA at 6.) To be defamatory, however, Plaintiff must also demonstrate that Defendants' statements are false and unprivileged. Parties should be prepared to discuss at the hearing whether Plaintiff has made a prima facie showing of falsity and the applicable legal standards to apply in determining whether to quash or issue Plaintiff's subpoena. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sfjudgeprotem@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
